prompt: Compare two versions of an application configuration schema to identify format drift. The old schema is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/schemas/v1.json' and the new schema is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/schemas/v2.json'. Both files define configuration keys with types, defaults, and descriptions. Your task: 1) Read both schema files completely. 2) For each configuration key present in either file, compare: field type (string vs number vs boolean), default value, whether the field is required or optional, description text, and any validation rules. 3) Categorize differences as: 'added' (new in v2), 'removed' (gone in v2), 'type_changed' (different type), 'default_changed' (different default), 'required_changed' (required vs optional flipped). 4) Generate a schema drift report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/schema-compare-report.txt' listing all differences with before/after values and a risk assessment for each change (breaking vs non-breaking).
